t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None  # or appropriate default value\n    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters: F={F}, K={K}, skipping Recommendations\n340\t\n341\t### Immediate Actions (Priority 1) 🔴\n342\t\n343\t#### 1. **Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options-Greeks** \n344\t**Priority:** CRITICAL  \n345\t**Business Impact:** Eliminates 127+ calculation errors, prevents 247+ restarts\n346\t**Timeline:** Implement immediately\n347\t\n348\t**Code Solution:**\n349\t```python\n350\tdef safe_implied_volatility_calculation(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag):\n351\t    \"\"\"\n352\t    Calculate implied volatility with input validation guards\n353\t    \"\"\"\n354\t    # Input validation guards\n355\t    if t <= 0:\n356\t        logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter: t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n357\t        return None\n358\t    \n359\t    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n360\t        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters: F={F}, K={K}, skipping calculation\")\n361\t        return None\n362\t    \n363\t    if undiscounted_option_price <= 0:\n364\t        logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price: {undiscounted_option_price}, skipping\")\n365\t        return None\n366\t    \n367\t    try:\n368\t        return vectorized_implied_volatility(\n369\t            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n370\t        )\n371\t    except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n372\t        logger.error(f\"Calculation failed: price={undiscounted_option_price}, \"\n373\t                    f\"F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}, error={e}\")\n374\t        return None\n375\t    except Exception as e:\n376\t        logger.error(f\"Unexpected calculation error: {e}\")\n377\t        return None\n378\t```\n379\t\n380\t**Testing Strategy:**\n381\t1. Test with historical data that triggered the errors\n382\t2. Add unit tests for edge cases (t=0, F=0, K=0)\n383\t3. Monitor error logs after deployment\n384\t4. Verify restart counts decrease\n385\t\n386\t#### 2. **Clean Up Failed Pods in Both Systems**\n387\t**Priority:** HIGH  \n388\t**Impact:** Improved operational hygiene, resource cleanup\n389\t\n390\t```bash\n391\t# Options pipeline - remove unknown status pod\n392\tkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 \\\n393\t  delete pod options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-8db6c -n ibkr-mcp-server-7dd7c9c9bc-6cn57 -n ibkr-mcp \\\n403\t  --force --grace-period=0\n404\t```\n405\t\n406\t### Medium-Term Improvements (Priority 2) 🟡\n407\t\n408\t#### 3. **Implement Comprehensive Input Validation Framework**\n409\t**Actions:**\n410\t- Add data quality checks before expensive calculations\n411\t- Create validation layer for options data processing\n412\t- Implement data quality metrics and monitoring\n413\t- Add schema validation for all input parameters\n414\t\n415\t**Validation Framework Example:**\n416\t```python\n417\tclass OptionsDataValidator:\n418\t    \"\"\"Validates options data before processing\"\"\"\n419\t    \n420\t    def validate_calculation_inputs(self, option_data):\n421\t        \"\"\"Validate inputs for greeks calculation\"\"\"\n422\t        errors = []\n423\t        \n424\t        if option_data.get('time_to_expiry', 0) <= 0:\n425\t            errors.append(f\"Invalid time_to_expiry: {option_data.get('time_to_expiry')}\")\n426\t        \n427\t        if option_data.get('forward_price', 0) <= 0:\n428\t            errors.append(f\"Invalid forward_price: {option_data.get('forward_price')}\")\n429\t        \n430\t        if option_data.get('strike_price', 0) <= 0:\n431\t            errors.append(f\"Invalid strike_price: {option_data.get('strike_price')}\")\n432\t        \n433\t        if errors:\n434\t            logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {errors}\")\n435\t            return False, errors\n436\t        \n437\t        return True, None\n438\t```\n439\t\n440\t#### 4. **Enhance Error Handling and Resilience**\n441\t**Implementation:**\n442\t```python\n443\tclass OptionsCalculator:\n444\t    def __init__(self):\n445\t        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n446\t        self.validator = OptionsDataValidator()\n447\t    \n448\t    def safe_calculate_greeks(self, option_data):\n449\t        \"\"\"Calculate greeks with comprehensive error handling\"\"\"\n450\t        try:\n451\t            # Validate inputs first\n452\t            is_valid, errors = self.validator.validate_calculation_inputs(option_data)\n453\t            if not is_valid:\n454\t                self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid inputs: {option_data.get('symbol')}, errors: {errors}\")\n455\t                return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n456\t            \n457\t            # Perform calculation\n458\t            return self.calculate_greeks(option_data)\n459\t            \n460\t        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n461\t            self.logger.error(f\"Calculation error for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n462\t            return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n463\t        except Exception as e:\n464\t            self.logger.error(f\"Unexpected error for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n465\t            return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n466\t    \n467\t    def get_default_greeks(self, option_data):\n468\t        \"\"\"Return default greeks for failed calculations\"\"\"\n469\t        return {\n470\t            'symbol': option_data.get('symbol'),\n471\t            'delta': 0.0,\n472\t            'gamma': 0.0,\n473\t            'theta': 0.0,\n474\t            'vega': 0.0,\n475\t            'calculation_status': 'failed',\n476\t            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat()\n477\t        }\n478\t```\n479\t\n480\t#### 5. **Improve External API Error Handling**\n481\t**Cloudflare API Integration Fix:**\n482\t```python\n483\tdef verify_cloudflare_deployment(deployment_id, max_retries=3):\n484\t    \"\"\"\n485\t    Verify Cloudflare deployment with improved error handling\n486\t    \"\"\"\n487\t    retry_count = 0\n488\t    \n489\t    while retry_count < max_retries:\n490\t        try:\n491\t            # Check if deployment exists first\n492\t            deployment = cf_client.get_deployment(deployment_id)\n493\t            if not deployment:\n494\t                logger.warning(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} not found, skipping verification\")\n495\t                return False\n496\t            \n497\t            # Verify deployment status\n498\t            if deployment['status'] == 'success':\n499\t                logger.info(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} verified successfully\")\n500\t                return True\n501\t            else:\n502\t                logger.warning(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} status: {deployment['status']}\")\n503\t                return False\n504\t                \n505\t        except HTTPError as e:\n506\t            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n507\t                retry_count += 1\n508\t                if retry_count >= max_retries:\n509\t                    logger.error(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} not found after {max_retries} retries\")\n510\t                    return False\n511\t                # Exponential backoff\n512\t                sleep_time = 2 ** retry_count\n513\t                logger.info(f\"Retry {retry_count}/{max_retries} after {sleep_time}s\")\n514\t                time.sleep(sleep_time)\n515\t            else:\n516\t                logger.error(f\"API error: {e}\")\n517\t                raise\n518\t```\n519\t\n520\t#### 6. **Add Comprehensive Monitoring and Alerting**\n521\t**Implementation:**\n522\t```python\n523\t# Metrics      'calculation_success_rate': 0.95,\n530\t        'external_api_errors': 0\n531\t    },\n532\t    'ibkr_mcp': {\n533\t        'health_check_latency_ms': 100,\n534\t        'connection_status': 'stable',\n535\t        'authentication_failures': 0,\n536\t        'pod_uptime_days': 9\n537\t    }\n538\t}\n539\t\n540\t# Alert thresholds\n541\talert_thresholds = {\n542\t    'warning': {\n543\t        'error_rate_per_hour': 5,\n544\t        'restart_count_increase': 10\n545\t    },\n546\t    'critical': {\n547\t        'error_rate_per_hour': 10,\n548\t        'restart_count_increase': 20,\n549\t        'health_check_latency_ms': 500\n550\t    }\n551\t}\n552\t```\n553\t\n554\t### Long-Term Architecture (Priority 3) 🟢\n555\t\n556\t#### 7. **Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern**\n557\t**Purpose:** Prevent cascade failures from external dependencies\n558\t\n559\t```python\n560\tclass CircuitBreaker:\n561\t    \"\"\"\n562\t    Circuit breaker for external API calls\n563\t    \"\"\"\n564\t    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=5, timeout=60):\n565\t        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n566\t        self.timeout = timeout\n567\t        self.failure_count = 0\n568\t        self.last_failure_time = None\n569\t        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n570\t    \n571\t    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n572\t        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n573\t        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n574\t            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n575\t                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n576\t                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n577\t            else:\n578\t                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN\")\n579\t        \n580\t        try:\n581\t            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n582\t            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n583\t                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n584\t                self.failure_count = 0\n585\t                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker reset to CLOSED state\")\n586\t            return result\n587\t        except Exception as e:\n588\t            self.failure_count += 1\n589\t            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n590\t            \n591\t            if self.failure_count >= self.failure_threshold:\n592\t                self.state = 'OPEN'\n593\t                logger.error(f\"Circuit breaker opened after {self.failure_count} failures\")\n594\t            \n595\t            raise\n596\t```\n597\t\n598\t#### 8. **Implement Dead Letter Queue Pattern**\n599\t**Purpose:** Route failed records for analysis instead of silent failure\n600\t\n601\t```python\n602\tclass DeadLetterQueue:\n603\t    \"\"\"\n604\t    Dead letter queue for failed calculations\n605\t    \"\"\"\n606\t    def __init__(self, max_size=1000):\n607\t        self.failed_records = []\n608\t        self.max_size = max_size\n609\t    \n610\t    def add_failed_record(self, record, error):\n611\t        \"\"\"Add failed record to DLQ\"\"\"\n612\t        if len(self.failed_records) >= self.max_size:\n613\t            logger.warning(\"DLQ at max capacity, dropping oldest record\")\n614\t            self.failed_records.pop(0)\n615\t        \n616\t        self.failed_records.append({\n617\t            'record': record,\n618\t            'error': str(error),\n619\t            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n620\t            'processed': False\n621\t        })\n622\t        logger.warning(f\"Added record to DLQ: {record.get('symbol')}, error: {error}\")\n623\t    \n624\t    def get_failed_records(self):\n625\t        \"\"\"Retrieve all failed records for analysis\"\"\"\n626\t        return [r for r in self.failed_records if not r['processed']]\n627\t    \n628\t    def mark_processed(self, record_id):\n629\t        \"\"\"Mark record as processed\"\"\"\n630\t        if 0 <= record_id < len(self.failed_records):\n631\t            self.failed_records[record_id]['processed'] = True\n632\t```\n633\t\n634\t#### 9. **Enhance Observability Infrastructure**\n635\t**Implementation prevents 247+ restarts\n**Timeline:** Implement immediately\n\n**Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef safe_implied_volatility_calculation(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag):\n    \"\"\"\n    Calculate implied volatility with input validation guards\n    \"\"\"\n    # Input validation guards\n    if t <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter: t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    \n    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters: F={F}, K={K}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    \n    if undiscounted_option_price <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price: {undiscounted_option_price}, skipping\")\n        return None\n    \n    try:\n        return vectorized_implied_volatility(\n            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n        )\n    except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Calculation failed: price={undiscounted_option_price}, \"\n                    f\"F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}, error={e}\")\n        return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Unexpected calculation error: {e}\")\n        return None\n```\n\n**Testing Strategy:**\n1. data quality metrics and monitoring\n- Add schema validation for all input parameters\n\n**Validation Framework Example:**\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Validates options data before processing\"\"\"\n    \n    def validate_calculation_inputs(self, option_data):\n        \"\"\"Validate inputs for greeks calculation\"\"\"\n        errors = []\n        \n        if option_data.get('time_to_expiry', 0) <= 0:\n            errors.append(f\"Invalid time_to_expiry: {option_data.get('time_to_expiry')}\")\n        \n        if option_data.get('forward_price', 0) <= 0:\n            errors.append(f\"Invalid forward_price: {option_data.get('forward_price')}\")\n        \n        if option_data.get('strike_price', 0) <= 0:\n            errors.append(f\"Invalid strike_price: {option_data.get('strike_price')}\")\n        \n        if errors:\n            logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {errors}\")\n            return False, errors\n        \n        return True, None\n```\n\n#### 4. **Enhance Error Handling and Resilience**\n**Implementation:**\n```python\nclass OptionsCalculator:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n        self.validator = OptionsDataValidator()\n    \n    def safe_calculate_greeks(self, option_data):\n        \"\"\"Calculate greeks with comprehensive error handling\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Validate inputs first\n            is_valid, errors = self.validator.validate_calculation_inputs(option_data)\n            if not is_valid:\n                self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid inputs: {option_data.get('symbol')}, errors: {errors}\")\n                return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n            \n            # Perform calculation\n            return self.calculate_greeks(option_data)\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f\"Calculation error for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f\"Unexpected error for {option_data.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            return self.get_default_greeks(option_data)\n    \n    def get_default_greeks(self, option_data):\n        \"\"\"Return default greeks for failed calculations\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'symbol': option_data.get('symbol'),\n            'delta': 0.0,\n            'gamma': 0.0,\n            'theta': 0.0,\n            'vega': 0.0,\n            'calculation_status': 'failed',\n            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat()\n        }\n```\n\n#### 5. **Improve External API Error Handling**\n**Cloudflare API Integration Fix:**\n```python\ndef verify_cloudflare_deployment(deployment_id, max_retries=3):\n    \"\"\"\n    Verify Cloudflare deployment with improved error handling\n    \"\"\"\n    retry_count = 0\n    \n    while retry_count < max_retries:\n        try:\n            # Check if deployment exists first\n            deployment = cf_client.get_deployment(deployment_id)\n            if not deployment:\n                logger.warning(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} not found, skipping verification\")\n                return False\n            \n            # Verify deployment status\n            if deployment['status'] == 'success':\n                logger.info(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} verified successfully\")\n                return True\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} status: {deployment['status']}\")\n                return False\n                \n        except HTTPError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                retry_count += 1\n                if retry_count >= max_retries:\n                    logger.error(f\"Deployment {deployment_id} not found after {max_retries} retries\")\n                    return False\n                # Exponential backoff\n                sleep_time = 2 ** retry_count\n                logger.info(f\"Retry {retry_count}/{max_retries} after {sleep_time}s\")\n                time.sleep(sleep_time)\n            else:\n                logger.error(f\"API error: {e}\")\n                raise\n```\n\n#### 6. **Add Comprehensive Monitoring and Alerting**\n**Implementation:**\n```python\n# Metrics to track\nmetrics_to_track = {\n    'options_pipeline': {\n        'error_rate_per_hour': 0,\n        'restart_count_total': 0,\n        'zerodivisionerror_count': 0,\n        'calculation_success_rate': 0.95,\n        'external_api_errors': 0\n    },\n    'ibkr_mcp': {\n        'health_check_latency_ms': 100,\n        'connection_status': 'stable',\n        'authentication_failures': 0,\n        'pod_uptime_days': 9\n    }\n}\n\n# Alert thresholds\nalert_thresholds = {\n    'warning': {\n        'error_rate_per_hour': 5,\n        'restart_count_increase': 10\n    },\n    'critical': {\n        'error_rate_per_hour': 10,\n        'restart_count_increase': 20,\n        'health_check_latency_ms': 500\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Long-Term Architecture (Priority 3) 🟢\n\n#### 7. **Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern**\n**Purpose:** Prevent cascade failures from external dependencies\n\n```python\nclass CircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"\n    Circuit breaker for external API calls\n    \"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=5, timeout=60):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.failure_count = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n                self.failure_count = 0\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker reset to CLOSED state\")\n            return result\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.failure_count += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            \n            if self.failure_count >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = 'OPEN'\n                logger.error(f\"Circuit breaker opened after {self.failure_count} failures\")\n            \n            raise\n```\n\n#### 8. **Implement Dead Letter Queue Pattern**\n**Purpose:** Route failed records for analysis instead of silent failure\n\n```python\nclass DeadLetterQueue:\n    \"\"\"\n    Dead letter queue for failed calculations\n    \"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, max_size=1000):\n        self.failed_records = []\n        self.max_size = max_size\n    \n    def add_failed_record(self, record, error):\n        \"\"\"Add failed record to DLQ\"\"\"\n        if len(self.failed_records) >= self.max_size:\n            logger.warning(\"DLQ at max capacity, dropping oldest record\")\n            self.failed_records.pop(0)\n        \n        self.failed_records.append({\n            'record': record,\n            'error': str(error),\n            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            'processed': False\n        })\n        logger.warning(f\"Added record to DLQ: {record.get('symbol')}, error: {error}\")\n    \n    def get_failed_records(self):\n        \"\"\"Retrieve all failed records for analysis\"\"\"\n        return [r for r in self.failed_records if not r['processed']]\n    \n    def mark_processed(self, record_id):\n        \"\"\"Mark record as processed\"\"\"\n        if 0 <= record_id < len(self.failed_records):\n            self.failed_records[record_id]['processed'] = True\n```\n\n#### 9. **Enhance Observability Infrastructure**\n**Implementation Plan:**\n- Deploy structured logging (JSON format) for both services\n- Set up Prometheus metrics for real-time monitoring  \n- Create Grafana dashboards for error visualization\n- Implement distributed tracing for request flow analysis\n- Add correlation IDs for tracing failures across systems\n\n---\n\n## Conclusions and Strategic Assessment\n\n### System Stability Assessment\n\n**Options Pipeline: 🔴 CRITICAL - Immediate Attention Required**\n- **Current State:** }\n74\t            }\n75\t        }\n76\t    }\n77\t    Ok(())\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\t// ── Rotating writer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n81\t\n82\tstruct RotatingFileWriter date: today,\n129\t            seq,\n130\t            dir,\n131\t        })\n132\t    }\n133\t\n134\t    fn rotate(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {\n135\t        let today = Local::now().date_naive();\n136\t        if self.date < today {\n137\t            self.date = today;\n138\t            self.seq = 0;\n139\t        } else {\n140\t            self.seq += 1;\n141\t        }\n142\t        let path = file_path(&self.dir, self.date, self.seq);\n143\t        self.file = File::create(&path)?;\n144\t        self.path = path;\n145\t        self.size = 0;\n146\t u64;\n162\t        Ok(buf.len())\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    fn flush(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {\n166\t        self.file.flush()\n167\t    }\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t// ── MakeWriter adapter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n171\t\n172\t/// Newtype wrapper because `MutexGuard<T>: Write` requires Rust ≥1.76.\n173\tstruct WriteGuard<'a>(std::sync::MutexGuard<'a, Rust `target/` dirs are by far the largest consumers — each can grow to 80–100G+ — and have filled the disk to 100% before, breaking every build on the machine.\n11\t\n12\t- **Before a large Rust build, check free space.** If the root filesystem has **less than ~20G free**, clear an *idle* `target/` first — preferably in a repo you are *not* actively building:\n13\t\n14\t  ```bash\n15\t  df -BG --output=avail / | tail -1        # free GB on /\n16\t  du -sh ~/*/target 2>/dev/null | sort -rh  # biggest target dirs\n17\t  rm -rf ~/<other-repo>/target              # regenerable; cargo rebuilds it\n18\t  ```\n19\t\n20\t- **Confirm a `target/` is idle before removing it:** `lsof +D ~/<repo>/target` shows nothing, and no `cargo`/`rustc` process references it.\n21\t- **Never back up `target/`** (no `target.bak`, `target.old.*`) — it is fully regenerable build output and only doubles disk use.\n22\t- **Do not reflexively clear `target/` on every repo switch** — that destroys incremental-compilation caches and forces slow cold rebuilds.        ON stitch_links(to_stitch)\n        ;\nCREATE TABLE patterns (\n            id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,\n            title TEXT NOT NULL,\n            description TEXT,\n            status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'planned'\n                CHECK(status IN ('planned', 'active', 'blocked', 'done', 'abandoned')),\n            owner TEXT,\n            deadline TEXT,\n            parent_pattern TEXT REFERENCES patterns(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n            created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n            updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n            CHECK(parent_pattern IS NULL OR parent_pattern != id)\n        );\nCREATE INDEX idx_patterns_status\n        ON patterns(status)\n        ;\nCREATE INDEX idx_patterns_parent\n        ON patterns(parent_pattern)\n        ;\nCREATE TRIGGER check_pattern_cycle\n        BEFORE UPDATE OF parent_pattern ON patterns\n        WHEN NEW.parent_pattern IS NOT NULL\n        BEGIN\n            SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'Pattern parent cycle detected')\n            WHERE EXISTS (\n                WITH RECURSIVE ancestors(ancestor_id) AS (\n                    SELECT NEW.parent_pattern\n                    UNION ALL\n                    SELECT p.parent_pattern\n                    FROM patterns p\n                    INNER JOIN ancestors a ON p.id = a.ancestor_id\n                    WHERE p.parent_pattern IS NOT NULL\n                )\n                SELECT 1 FROM ancestors WHERE ancestor_id = NEW.id\n            );\n        END;\nCREATE TRIGGER check_pattern_self_ref_insert\n        BEFORE INSERT ON patterns\n        WHEN NEW.parent_pattern IS NOT NULL\n        BEGIN\n            SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'Pattern cannot reference itself as parent')\n            WHERE EXISTS (\n                SELECT 1 WHERE NEW.parent_pattern = NEW.id\n            );\n        END;\nCREATE TABLE pattern_members (\n            pattern_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES patterns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n            stitch_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stitches(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n            added_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n            PRIMARY KEY (pattern_id, stitch_id)\n
